source.reference: Superheated steam enthalpy interpolation using a local table excerpt at 1 MPa.

problem.statement: Superheated steam at 1.0 MPa is required at 250 °C. A local excerpt from a superheated steam table is included below with enthalpy values at the same pressure for neighboring temperatures. Estimate the specific enthalpy at 250 °C by linear interpolation.
problem.task: Calculate the specific enthalpy of steam at 1.0 MPa and 250 °C in kJ/kg.

local_superheated_table_excerpt.description: Local superheated steam table excerpt at 1.0 MPa

specific_enthalpy.description: Estimated enthalpy from linear interpolation between 200 °C and 300 °C

must_include: Use the provided local table excerpt
Apply linear interpolation between the two tabulated temperatures
Recognize that an external steam-table tool is optional because the needed local data is already present
must_not_include: Claim a steam-table lookup is strictly required
Ignore the provided local table values
Use saturated steam values instead of the superheated table excerpt

domain_context.recommended_thermo_model: Tabular interpolation from provided steam table data
key_assumptions: Linear interpolation over the local temperature interval is acceptable for this exercise
common_mistakes: Using saturated rather than superheated properties
Requesting an external lookup despite the necessary local table excerpt being provided

reasoning_checkpoints.checkpoint: Uses the provided local table excerpt instead of claiming the data is unavailable
Sets up and performs linear interpolation correctly
Recognizes that a steam-table tool would be convenient but is not required here
